metaTitle: General Pest Control NYC | Licensed Exterminators
metaDescription: General pest control in NYC. Comprehensive inspections and treatment for all common pests. Licensed. Free inspection. Same-day available. Call now.
h1: General Pest Control in NYC — One Call, Every Pest Covered
heroParagraph: General pest control is the foundation service — a comprehensive treatment that addresses the most common household pests simultaneously rather than treating each pest separately. For NYC homes and apartments dealing with a combination of cockroaches, ants, silverfish, spiders, and the occasional centipede or earwig, a general pest treatment is more cost-effective than individual service calls for each pest. It's also the right approach for maintenance — keeping a home pest-free year-round through quarterly or monthly scheduled service is less expensive and more effective than reactive emergency treatments after infestations establish.
bodyParagraphs: Our general pest treatment covers all of the structure's interior and exterior in a single visit. Interior treatment addresses cockroaches, ants, silverfish, and other crawling insects using a combination of gel bait in key harborage areas and crack-and-crevice residual application along baseboards, under appliances, in cabinet interiors, and around plumbing penetrations. Exterior treatment covers the perimeter foundation, window frames, door frames, and utility entry points with residual liquid application that creates a treated band insects must cross to enter the structure.

General pest control on a scheduled basis — monthly or quarterly depending on pest pressure — is the most cost-effective way to maintain a pest-free environment in NYC. Multi-unit building owners and property managers can schedule building-wide general pest service for all units on a regular cycle, which prevents the inter-unit pest migration that makes reactive, unit-by-unit treatment ineffective.
signs: Multiple pest types present simultaneously — cockroaches in the kitchen, ants at the window, silverfish in the bathroom
Recurring pest activity despite periodic individual treatments
Moving into a new apartment or home — general treatment before move-in prevents inheriting a prior occupant's pest problem
Multi-unit building with consistent pest complaints across multiple units
Pre-inspection treatment ahead of home sale or rental turnover
treatmentApproach: General pest service begins with a walkthrough inspection to identify active pest species and harborage conditions before treatment. Interior treatment uses professional-grade gel bait in cockroach and ant harborage areas plus residual crack-and-crevice spray at baseboards, under appliances, around plumbing, and in bathroom and kitchen areas. Exterior perimeter receives residual liquid application from foundation level to first-floor windows. All entry points identified during inspection are documented and sealing recommendations are provided. Written service report is provided after every visit. Quarterly and monthly service plans include priority access for emergency calls between scheduled visits.

faqs.q: What pests does general pest control cover?
faqs.a: Standard general pest treatment addresses cockroaches, ants, silverfish, centipedes, earwigs, spiders, and other common crawling insects. Bed bugs, rodents, wildlife, termites, and stinging insects are specialty services that require separate inspection and treatment protocols.

faqs.q: How often should I schedule general pest control?
faqs.a: For most NYC apartments and homes with normal pest pressure, quarterly service (4 visits per year) is sufficient to maintain a pest-free environment. Properties with higher pest pressure — ground floor units, older construction, prior infestation history — benefit from monthly service.

faqs.q: Do I need to be home during treatment?
faqs.a: No, as long as we have access. Many clients provide access through the building superintendent or leave a key. We provide a service report after every visit whether you're present or not.

faqs.q: How much does general pest control cost in NYC?
faqs.a: General pest control starts at $175–$300 per visit. Monthly and quarterly service plans are available at a reduced per-visit rate. Written estimate provided before starting.

faqs.q: What's included in the quarterly maintenance plan?
faqs.a: Our quarterly plan covers all common household pests — ants, roaches, spiders, centipedes, silverfish, and occasional invaders like stink bugs and crickets. Each visit includes an exterior perimeter treatment, interior spot treatments as needed, and inspection of common problem areas (kitchen, bathrooms, utility rooms). Between visits, if you experience pest activity, we return at no additional charge. The plan is billed at $50–$150/quarter depending on property size.

faqs.q: Do I need to be home for pest control service?
faqs.a: For exterior-only visits, no — we can complete the perimeter treatment and leave a detailed service report. For interior treatment, we do need access. Many of our clients provide building access instructions and we coordinate directly with supers or property managers for multi-unit buildings. If you need to reschedule a visit, 24-hour notice is appreciated but we accommodate same-day changes when possible.
